channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How much is the war hitting American's bottom line?
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Martha Gimbel, executive director and co-founder of the Budget Lab at Yale, about living in a war economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Martha Gimbel, executive director and co-founder of the Budget Lab at Yale, about living in a war economy.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you
item.description: With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.
item.content:encoded:
With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
item.description: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Life Kit team shares tax choices that save you money
item.description: Life Kit has tips to help you make better tax choices now that'll save you money in 2026.
item.content:encoded: Life Kit has tips to help you make better tax choices now that'll save you money in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: What's next for oil markets after the price of oil hit a 4-year high
item.description: Supply shocks are driving up the price of oil. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Kevin Book from ClearView Energy Partners about how the war in Iran is impacting the oil market.
item.content:encoded: Supply shocks are driving up the price of oil. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Kevin Book from ClearView Energy Partners about how the war in Iran is impacting the oil market.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
item.description: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.
item.content:encoded:
After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.

(Image credit: Daniel Lozada for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Federal debt exceeds $39 trillion for the 1st time. Why is this milestone significant?
item.description: The federal debt passed an uncomfortable milestone this year, outgrowing the entire U.S. economy. That poses risks, but policymakers show little sign of addressing it.
item.content:encoded: The federal debt passed an uncomfortable milestone this year, outgrowing the entire U.S. economy. That poses risks, but policymakers show little sign of addressing it.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Iran war shakes Global economy as energy costs surge and recession fears grow
item.description: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.content:encoded: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: The effects of the Iran war on Americans' wallet
item.description: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: The Iran war has caused oil and gas prices to skyrocket. How it's affecting consumers
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
